dictionary.definition: Stats are the six core abilities of your hero: Strength (physical power), Dexterity (agility), Constitution (endurance), Intelligence (reasoning), Wisdom (perception), and Charisma (influence). Stats affect skill checks, combat, and saves.
Skills are specialized abilities your hero uses to perform tasks. Examples include Athletics, Stealth, Persuasion, and Arcana. Skill checks use a d20 plus relevant modifiers.
A roll to determine success in a task. Roll a d20, add skill and stat modifiers, and compare to the GM’s difficulty. Used for actions like climbing, sneaking, or persuading.
A roll to resist harmful effects (like poison or spells). Roll a d20, add relevant stat modifier, and compare to the effect’s difficulty.
Hit Points (HP) measure your hero’s health. When reduced to zero, your hero is dying and must make saves to avoid death. Healing restores HP.
Speed is how far your hero can move on their turn, usually measured in feet or squares. Movement can be split before and after actions.
Range is the maximum distance for attacks, spells, or abilities. Ranged weapons and spells list their range in feet or squares.
A major action taken on your turn in combat, such as attacking, casting a spell, helping an ally, or using an item. Each turn, you can take one Heroic Action.
A special response to threats during combat, like dodging an attack, countering a spell, or protecting a friend. Reactions are limited and often require specific triggers.
The process of building your hero: choose a class, ancestry, background, assign stats, select skills, and equip items. Fill out your character sheet as you go.
Items your hero carries, including armor, weapons, tools, and magical items. Equipment affects combat, defense, and abilities.
Protective gear that reduces damage taken. Types include light, medium, and heavy armor, each with different defense bonuses and movement penalties.
Weapons used in close combat, such as swords, axes, and maces. Melee attacks use Strength or Dexterity modifiers.
Weapons used to attack from a distance, like bows, crossbows, and slings. Ranged attacks use Dexterity modifiers and have a listed range.
Special items with magical properties, such as enchanted weapons, armor, potions, or artifacts. Magical items grant bonuses or abilities.
Magical abilities heroes can cast. Spells are divided into types: Fire, Ice, Lightning, Wind, Radiant, Necrotic, and Utility. Each spell has effects, range, and requirements.
A scroll containing a spell that can be cast once. After use, the scroll is consumed.
A magical item used to cast spells. Wands may have charges or limited uses.
A period of downtime where heroes recover hit points, abilities, and spells. Short rests restore some resources; long rests restore most.
Time spent between adventures for training, crafting, socializing, or pursuing personal goals. Downtime activities can grant bonuses or new abilities.
Additional rules that can be used at the GM’s discretion, such as measuring spaces, special conditions, or custom rulings.
A state affecting your hero, such as stunned, poisoned, grappled, blinded, or restrained. Conditions impact actions, movement, and combat.
A bonus to defense when hiding behind objects. Cover reduces the chance of being hit by attacks or spells.
Restraining an opponent in combat. Grappled creatures have limited movement and may need to escape with a skill check.
Your hero’s origin or race, such as human, elf, dwarf, or exotic ancestries. Ancestry affects stats, abilities, and traits.
Your hero’s history or profession before adventuring. Background grants skills, abilities, and motivation.
The reason your hero seeks adventure, such as fame, fortune, justice, or curiosity. Motivation shapes roleplay and decisions.
Your hero’s role, such as warrior, mage, rogue, cleric, etc. Class determines abilities, spells, and combat style.
Increasing your hero’s abilities and power by gaining experience. Leveling up grants new skills, spells, and bonuses.
A spell dealing fire damage or effects, such as burning enemies or creating flames.
A spell dealing ice damage or effects, such as freezing targets or creating barriers.
A spell dealing lightning damage or effects, such as shocking enemies or energizing allies.
A spell dealing wind damage or effects, such as pushing targets or creating gusts.
A spell dealing radiant (holy) damage or effects, such as healing or banishing undead.
A spell dealing necrotic (death) damage or effects, such as draining life or causing decay.
A spell with non-combat effects, like healing, teleportation, or enhancing abilities.
A list of terms and their definitions used in Nimble. Refer to the glossary for quick explanations of game concepts.
The physical scale of your hero or creature, affecting movement, reach, and certain abilities. Sizes include Small, Medium, Large, etc.
Maintaining focus on a spell or effect. If concentration is broken, the effect ends. Some spells require concentration to stay active.
Using objects or terrain to avoid detection or attacks. Provides bonuses to defense and stealth checks.
Monsters have their own stats, abilities, and armor ratings. Armor reduces damage and affects how hard a monster is to hit.
The process of beginning a combat encounter: determine initiative, place heroes and monsters, and start turns.
Periods between adventures for recovery, training, and personal pursuits. Resting restores health and abilities; downtime allows for growth.
A record of your hero’s stats, skills, abilities, equipment, and progress. Used to track all aspects of your character.
Standard races available for heroes, such as human, elf, dwarf, halfling, etc.
Rare or unusual races, offering unique abilities and traits. Examples include dragonborn, tiefling, or custom ancestries.
Pre-adventuring professions or histories that grant skills, abilities, and roleplay hooks.
Essential items for adventuring, such as rope, torches, healing potions, or spell components.
Additional gear for exploration and survival, like tents, maps, or tools.
Items imbued with magical power, granting special abilities or bonuses.
Items used to cast spells, either once (scrolls) or multiple times (wands).
Rules for determining distances, areas, and movement on maps or battlefields.
